responseMessageContents.text: Now let me wire up the delete button in the my-equipment screen:

edits.oldString:

responseMessageContents.text:
All done. Here's the summary:

**Files changed:**

1. **`expo/services/cloudinaryService.ts`** — Added `deleteCloudinaryImage()` and `deleteMultipleCloudinaryImages()` that call the Cloudflare Worker at `POST /cloudinary/delete`

2. **`expo/utils/imageHelpers.ts`** — Added `extractPublicIds()` (extracts all valid publicIds from an image array) and `getRemovedImages()` (diffs old vs new images to find removed publicIds for cleanup during updates)

3. **`expo/services/firestoreService.ts`** — Added `deleteEquipmentWithCleanup()` (fetches equipment, extracts publicIds, deletes from Cloudinary via Worker, then deletes Firestore doc) and `updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup()` (diffs old/new images, deletes only removed ones from Cloudinary, then updates Firestore)

4. **`expo/app/my-equipment.tsx`** — Wired the delete button with confirmation dialog, loading state, Cloudinary cleanup via `deleteEquipmentWithCleanup()`, and optimistic UI removal on success

5. **`expo/i18n/translations.ts`** — Added Arabic/English keys: `confirm_delete`, `confirm_delete_equipment`, `deleting`, `delete_success`, `delete_failed`

**What is now fully working:**
- Deleting equipment from "My Equipment" screen triggers Cloudinary image deletion through the Worker before removing the Firestore document
- `updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup()` is ready for any future edit screen to clean up replaced images

**Legacy image string handling:**
- `extractPublicIds()` and `getRemovedImages()` safely skip plain string entries — only `{ url, publicId }` objects with a valid `publicId` trigger Worker deletion calls

**Remaining limitations:**
- If the Worker call fails for some images, the Firestore document is still deleted (graceful degradation — orphaned Cloudinary assets may remain but the app won't crash)
- The edit equipment screen doesn't exist yet as a dedicated screen, but `updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup()` is ready to plug in when it does

responseMessageContents.title: Enable deleting equipment and removing its images from the app.
responseMessageContents.description: Users can now delete their equipment listings from the "My Equipment" section. When a listing is deleted, the app will also automatically clean up and remove the associated photos from storage. Added confirmation messages to prevent accidental deletions and updated the app to support both Arabic and English for these new actions.
